title: Solving Network Congestion in Dense Sensor Arrays: The Edge Computing Paradigm
subtitle: How decentralized processing and light communication models eliminate data queuing and unlock large N array scalability.
description: As dense sensor networks grow, transmitting raw data to centralized cloud servers causes severe network congestion and queuing. Discover how edge computing and light communication models solve this bottleneck.
excerpt: When thousands of sensors stream data simultaneously, networks choke and data queues up. Edge computing solves this by processing data locally and transmitting only lightweight insights, fundamentally changing large N array architecture.
hero_caption: Edge processing nodes filtering raw data and transmitting lightweight insights to prevent network congestion.
hero_aria: Diagram illustrating dense sensor arrays using edge computing to process data locally and avoiding network bottlenecks
